title: hieracium bupleuroides subsp. calycinum
intro: Hieracium bupleuroides subsp. calycinum, also known as hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and roots, which contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that may help alleviate digestive issues and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Hieracium bupleuroides subsp. calycinum include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and support the immune system, as well as its use as a diuretic and to treat skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
